title: salix purpurea subsp. amplexicaulis
intro: Salix purpurea subsp. amplexicaulis, also known as purple osier, is a subspecies of the purple willow (Salix purpurea) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Salix purpurea subsp. amplexicaulis include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
